unit.comment: /*************************************************************************** * _ _ ____ _ * Project ___| | | | _ \| | * / __| | | | |_) | | * | (__| |_| | _ <| |___ * \___|\___/|_| \_\_____| * * Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al. * * This software is licensed as described in the file COPYING, which * you should have received as part of this distribution. The terms * are also available at https://curl.se/docs/copyright.html. * * You may opt to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute and/or sell * copies of the Software, and permit persons to whom the Software is * furnished to do so, under the terms of the COPYING file. * * This software is distributed on an "AS IS" basis, WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY * KIND, either express or implied. * * SPDX-License-Identifier: curl * ***************************************************************************/
unit.cpp:error: # error "Can't compile NTLM support without a crypto library with DES."
unit.comment: /* The last 3 #include files should be in this order */

function.name: extend_key_56_to_64

unit.comment: /* * Turns a 56 bit key into the 64 bit, odd parity key and sets the key. The * key schedule ks is also set. */

function.name: setup_des_key
setup_des_key
encrypt_des
function.parameter_list: (const unsigned char *in, unsigned char *out, const unsigned char *key_56)

function.name: encrypt_des
function.parameter_list: (const unsigned char *in, unsigned char *out, const unsigned char *key_56)

function.name: encrypt_des
function.parameter_list: (const unsigned char *in, unsigned char *out, const unsigned char *key_56)

function.name: encrypt_des
function.parameter_list: (const unsigned char *in, unsigned char *out, const unsigned char *key_56)

unit.comment: /* * takes a 21 byte array and treats it as 3 56-bit DES keys. The * 8 byte plaintext is encrypted with each key and the resulting 24 * bytes are stored in the results array. */

function.name: Curl_ntlm_core_lm_resp
Curl_ntlm_core_mk_lm_hash
ascii_to_unicode_le
ascii_uppercase_to_unicode_le
Curl_ntlm_core_mk_nt_hash

unit.comment: /* Timestamp in tenths of a microsecond since January 1, 1601 00:00:00 UTC. */
/* Convert a time_t to an MS FILETIME (MS-DTYP section 2.3.3). */

function.name: time2filetime
Curl_ntlm_core_mk_ntlmv2_hash
function.parameter_list: (const char *user, size_t userlen, const char *domain, size_t domlen, unsigned char *ntlmhash, unsigned char *ntlmv2hash)

unit.comment: /* * Curl_ntlm_core_mk_ntlmv2_resp() * * This creates the NTLMv2 response as set in the ntlm type-3 message. * * Parameters: * * ntlmv2hash [in] - The ntlmv2 hash (16 bytes) * challenge_client [in] - The client nonce (8 bytes) * ntlm [in] - The ntlm data struct being used to read TargetInfo and Server challenge received in the type-2 message * ntresp [out] - The address where a pointer to newly allocated * memory holding the NTLMv2 response. * ntresp_len [out] - The length of the output message. * * Returns CURLE_OK on success. */

function.name: Curl_ntlm_core_mk_ntlmv2_resp
function.parameter_list: (unsigned char *ntlmv2hash, unsigned char *challenge_client, struct ntlmdata *ntlm, unsigned char **ntresp, unsigned int *ntresp_len)

unit.comment: /* * Curl_ntlm_core_mk_lmv2_resp() * * This creates the LMv2 response as used in the ntlm type-3 message. * * Parameters: * * ntlmv2hash [in] - The ntlmv2 hash (16 bytes) * challenge_client [in] - The client nonce (8 bytes) * challenge_client [in] - The server challenge (8 bytes) * lmresp [out] - The LMv2 response (24 bytes) * * Returns CURLE_OK on success. */

function.name: Curl_ntlm_core_mk_lmv2_resp
function.parameter_list: (unsigned char *ntlmv2hash, unsigned char *challenge_client, unsigned char *challenge_server, unsigned char *lmresp)
